title: trisetum spicatum subsp. tibeticum
intro: Trisetum spicatum subsp. tibeticum, also known as the Tibetan hair grass, is a subspecies of the Trisetum spicatum grass, a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is traditionally used in Tibetan medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Trisetum spicatum subsp. tibeticum include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
